site.strapline: Code is cheap. Show me the spec.
site.title: ADSM and TeqFW Ecosystem

footer.statement: public proof product for controlled agent-driven JavaScript evolution

hero.title: Build JavaScript products that agents can safely evolve.

pages.intro: For product owners, technical founders, and product-oriented engineers using LLM agents on real JavaScript products where durable control is now the problem.
pages.summary: Visitor-centered homepage for controlled JavaScript product evolution with LLM agents, the ADSM book, and inspectable public proof.
pages.title: TeqFW

hero.title: Keep repeated agent changes under product control.

pages.intro: Agents can already write code. The harder problem is keeping product meaning, boundaries, and continuity intact after repeated changes. ADSM, Agent Driven Software Management, is the control layer around repeated LLM-agent work.
pages.summary: Visitor-centered explanation of ADSM as the control layer around repeated LLM-agent work.
pages.title: Method

hero.title: Map the control stack around agent work.

pages.intro: ADSM, TeqFW, GitHub Flows, public proof, the book, and author practice are not separate assets. They are different control surfaces around the same problem: keeping JavaScript product evolution legible after repeated LLM-agent work.
pages.summary: Map how ADSM, TeqFW, GitHub Flows, public proof, book materials, and authorship work together as a control stack around agent-driven JavaScript product evolution.
pages.title: Ecosystem

hero.title: TeqFW is a philosophy for JavaScript web applications built and evolved with LLM agents.

pages.intro: A compact orientation to the architectural principles behind TeqFW, with a link to the maintained source document.
pages.summary: Compact entry point to the TeqFW architectural philosophy for modular monolith web applications in the era of LLM agents.
pages.title: Ecosystem Philosophy

hero.title: Can LLM agents safely evolve a real JavaScript product?

pages.intro: LLM agents can generate code quickly. The hard part is keeping the product controllable after repeated changes. This page shows public artifacts you can inspect before trusting the method.
pages.summary: Visitor-centered verification page for inspecting public evidence before trusting controlled agent-driven JavaScript evolution.
pages.title: Proof Verification

hero.title: Contact Alex Gusev about TeqFW, ADSM, and ecosystem materials.

pages.intro: Use these channels for questions about the ecosystem, book-related communication, starter support, paid materials, invoicing, or formal company matters.
pages.summary: Compact visitor-centered contact page for TeqFW, ADSM, GitHub Flows, ecosystem materials, formal company details, and a restrained boundary.
pages.title: Contacts
